comment.body: I don’t think he is personally taking the money. The dude is holed up in the presidential palace which is surrounded by sandbags. Not a pleasant living situation. Plus he was rich before the war. He was one of the biggest celebs in Ukraine. Other guys are probably taking money though. They were even before the war. You have to understand the culture there. There is no ownership of anything. No sense of community. Every person takes what they can get. You can see this even with their buildings. Most look like shit from the outside and in the common hallways because there is no social responsibility, but inside, they are nice and modern.

comment.body: The aggregate demand curve shifts down because people have less disposable income (negative wealth effect as referenced here) and therefore buy less stuff. Companies put stuff on sale to compete for the remaining purchase power (=> falling prices), lay off people (=> lower income, lower demand)

user.name: Stevo
user.bio: option learner.

comment.body:
Thinking contextually/critically and in a non binary view is something that isn’t necessarily hard for me. The real question is, what is the objective here? To build a trading plan around analysis that won’t be around after a year? Or to learn some skills where you can trade independently from the analysis and be self sustainable after a year by building that analysis yourself?

Would like some of the latter. But it is a good start to first be able to at least implement the analysis into an actionable trading plan. And that is still a tough task.

Now, as I’ve mentioned, Alma has natural talent… and talent, as I’ve said, is valued because it isn’t easily replicated. 

And Alma has been mentored, probably one on one, by some very talented people, in a setting where the screens were in front of her and she could actually learn on the fly, hands on.

So that’s something to keep in mind here is all I’m saying. It seems inevitable that if one becomes too complacent on Alma’s analysis, without understanding how to create it for yourself, then Alma goes from being a teacher, to an over nurturing “mommy”.

comment.body: One thing that few people understand: if you have ever tasted the sweet fruit of freedom / democracy / capitalism, even in a rudimentary form, you don't want to go back to the Russian system. Full shops all day vs. queing for a loaf of bread at 2 AM, the complete elimination of the national identity, a sytem based on extortion and power, this is ingrained in peoples brains. It tells a lot that Ukrainians would rather die than live under another Russian occupation. That's the part that people in the West don't get...
